draft.introduction: City of God Book 22, the final book, describes the eternal happiness of the saints in the resurrection. Augustine envisions the resurrected body perfected in beauty and the beatific vision as the ultimate fulfillment of human desire.

translation.text: There we shall rest and see, see and love, love and praise. This is what shall be in the end without end. For what other end do we have, if not to reach that kingdom which has no end? There we shall be still and know, know and love, love and praise. Behold what shall be in the end that is no end.
translation.original_text: Ibi vacabimus et videbimus, videbimus et amabimus, amabimus et laudabimus. Ecce quod erit in fine sine fine.

translator_notes.note: The cadence vacabimus et videbimus, videbimus et amabimus, amabimus et laudabimus (we shall rest and see, see and love, love and praise) achieves its power through cumulative progression. Each faculty enables the next: rest permits vision, vision kindles love, love overflows in praise.
